{{Quote|As I sat watching the howlrunners, the walls of the safari dome seemed to melt away. For a brief moment, I was part of the pack, hunting and howling with these magnificent predators. I have spent the rest of my life trying to recapture that feeling, from the cockpit of a starfighter.|Excerpt from ''The Anthropology of Engineering'' by Jo Ewsli|<ref name="NEGVV">''[[The New Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref>}}
The [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]] '''I-7 Howlrunner''' was a short range attack [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] employed by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|The I-7s have full robotic control. They make excellent guided missiles!|Kam Solusar to Rayf Ysanna|<ref name="ee1">[[Empire's End 1|''Empire's End'' 1]]</ref>}}
The Howlrunner was constructed around an aerodynamic fixed-wing structure. Two fins jutted out of the sides of the craft, meant to function as rudimentary maneuvering flaps.<ref name="NEGVV" /> Though this design was rather simple it gave the Howlrunner superior performance to the [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]] in both space and atmosphere. Around [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], they cost 165,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="SOG">''[[Starships of the Galaxy]]''</ref>

[[File:Howlrunner_egvv.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Howlrunner [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]].]]
The controls had a small learning curve, ensuring that nearly any [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] with basic skills could control the craft—although only the most experienced could discover the true power of this starfighter. While the Howlrunner was faster than the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]] or [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighter]], it was unable to match the speed of newer fighters such as the [[E-7 E-wing multi-role starfighter|E-wing escort starfighter]].<ref name="EGVV">''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref>

The Howlrunner had a distinct advantage over the Empire's [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]] in that it possessed an onboard [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shield]] generator.<ref name="EGVV" /> This protection, coupled with the starfighter's tiny profile and low mass, made the Howlrunner a hard ship to destroy. However, the craft was not meant for long space battles or pitched one-on-one combat.<ref name="NEGVV" />

The Howlrunner's twin laser cannons were relatively weak, and while they were quite accurate, this was hindered by the fighter's rudimentary targeting system.<ref name="NEGVV" /> In order to offset these weaknesses, the Empire sent multiple Howlrunners at an enemy all at once, hoping that the sheer volume of fire would be too much for their targets. As this was similar to the tactical doctrine used for TIE fighters, little adjustment was required to integrate the Howlrunner into Imperial squadrons.

==History==
[[File:BirthOfTheHowlRunner.jpg|left|thumb|A Howlrunner assembly line on [[Fresia/Legends|Fresia]].]]
The I-7 Howlrunner was originally conceived of and designed by starfighter engineer [[Jo Ewsli]]. After receiving an epiphany during a viewing of a pack of [[Howlrunner/Legends|howlrunners]], dangerous carnivores from the planet [[Kamar/Legends|Kamar]], Ewsli set out to create a spacecraft that emulated their unique predatory instincts. The creatures attacked in tight packs and were unyielding in their pursuit of prey, two abilities that Ewsli thought could be incorporated into a squadron with the right ships. In this vein, Ewsli unveiled a starfighter that, like its beastly namesake, had superb agility and speed.<ref name="NEGVV" />

The Howlrunner was the first starfighter designed and mass produced by [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]] after the X-wing defection fiasco. The main point of building the Howlrunner in the first place was an attempt to divert attention from the company's failure to stop the defection of the X-wing design team and the theft of the fighter's schematics.

However, the defection of the X-wing designers left Incom very unpopular among the Imperial ranks, and Howlrunners were mainly assigned to older starships and remote bases. It was a rare sight indeed to find Howlrunners in the hangar of an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]], though it was not unusual for [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]] to carry them. Though the Imperial Navy was unimpressed by it, there were rumors that some of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] advisers purchased their own Howlrunners for "personal business".<ref name="EGVV" /> This may have included [[Kam Solusar]] during his time as a member of the [[Dark Side Elite]], as he brought a two-seat variant of the Howlrunner with him when he defected to [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] nascent [[New Jedi Order|Jedi Order]].

Although it was not an exceptional fighter, the Howlrunner was often seen at remote Imperial bases where its speed allowed for rapid reconnaissance when necessary. These bases welcomed the Howlrunner, as they usually had no other modern starfighters, having to make due with early TIE/LN fighters. While the Empire lost most of its TIEs during the Galactic Civil War, as well as losing access to Sienar's production facilities, the Howlrunner line escaped destruction and found itself becoming one of the prime starfighters in the fleets of the [[Imperial Remnant]].

These craft continued to be used even after the Empire fractured and split into many warring factions. The Howlrunner was also used by the pilots of [[Saba Sebatyne]]'s [[Wild Knights]] squadron, during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] by the extragalactic [[Yuuzhan Vong]].<ref name="NEGVV" />

In the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], a number of them were in the [[Corellian Defense Force/Legends|Corellian Defense Force]].
